en.%markerMenu_marker_add_description%: For a translational addition to the text
en.%markerMenu_marker_addpn_description%: For chinese words to be dot underline & underline
en.%markerMenu_marker_b_description%: Poetry text stanza break (e.g. stanza break) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_bd_description%: A character style, use bold text
en.%markerMenu_marker_bdit_description%: A character style, use bold + italic text
en.%markerMenu_marker_bk_description%: For the quoted name of a book
en.%markerMenu_marker_ca_description%: Second (alternate) chapter number (for coding dual versification; useful for places where different traditions of chapter breaks need to be supported in the same translation)
en.%markerMenu_marker_cd_description%: Chapter Description (Publishing option D, e.g. in Russian Bibles)
en.%markerMenu_marker_cl_description%: Chapter label used for translations that add a word such as 'Chapter' before chapter numbers (e.g. Psalms). The subsequent text is the chapter label.
en.%markerMenu_marker_cp_description%: Published chapter number (chapter string that should appear in the published text)
en.%markerMenu_marker_d_description%: A Hebrew text heading, to provide description (e.g. Psalms)
en.%markerMenu_marker_dc_description%: Deuterocanonical/LXX additions or insertions in the Protocanonical text
en.%markerMenu_marker_em_description%: A character style, use emphasized text style
en.%markerMenu_marker_fdc_description%: Footnote text, applies to Deuterocanon only
en.%markerMenu_marker_fl_description%: A footnote label text item, for marking or 'labelling' the type or alternate translation being provided in the note.
en.%markerMenu_marker_fm_description%: An additional footnote marker location for a previous footnote
en.%markerMenu_marker_fq_description%: A footnote scripture quote or alternate rendering (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_fqa_description%: A footnote alternate rendering for a portion of scripture text
en.%markerMenu_marker_fr_description%: The origin reference for the footnote (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_fv_description%: A verse number within the footnote text
en.%markerMenu_marker_fw_description%: A footnote witness list, for distinguishing a list of sigla representing witnesses in critical editions.
en.%markerMenu_marker_h_description%: Running header text for a book (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_h2_description%: Running header text, left side of page
en.%markerMenu_marker_h3_description%: Running header text, right side of page
en.%markerMenu_marker_id_description%: File identification information (BOOKID, FILENAME, EDITOR, MODIFICATION DATE)
en.%markerMenu_marker_iex_description%: Introduction explanatory or bridge text (e.g. explanation of missing book in Short Old Testament)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ili_description%: A list entry, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ili1_description%: A list entry, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_im_description%: Introduction prose paragraph, with no first line indent (may occur after poetry)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imi_description%: Introduction prose paragraph text, indented, with no first line indent
en.%markerMenu_marker_imq_description%: Introduction prose paragraph, quote from the body text, with no first line indent
en.%markerMenu_marker_imt_description%: Introduction major title, level 1 (if single level) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imt1_description%: Introduction major title, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imt4_description%: Introduction major title, level 4 (usually within parenthesis)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imte_description%: Introduction major title at introduction end, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imte1_description%: Introduction major title at introduction end, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_imte2_description%: Introduction major title at introduction end, level 2
en.%markerMenu_marker_io_description%: Introduction outline text, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_io1_description%: Introduction outline text, level 1 (if multiple levels) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ior_description%: Introduction references range for outline entry; for marking references separately
en.%markerMenu_marker_ipi_description%: Introduction prose paragraph, indented, with first line indent
en.%markerMenu_marker_ipq_description%: Introduction prose paragraph, quote from the body text
en.%markerMenu_marker_iq_description%: Introduction poetry text, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_iq1_description%: Introduction poetry text, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_iqt_description%: For quoted scripture text appearing in the introduction
en.%markerMenu_marker_is_description%: Introduction section heading, level 1 (if single level) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_is1_description%: Introduction section heading, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_it_description%: A character style, use italic text
en.%markerMenu_marker_li_description%: A list entry, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_li1_description%: A list entry, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_lim_description%: An embedded list entry, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_lim1_description%: An embedded list entry, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_lit_description%: For a comment or note inserted for liturgical use
en.%markerMenu_marker_liv_description%: Structured list entry value 1 content (if single value)
en.%markerMenu_marker_liv1_description%: Structured list entry value 1 content (if multiple values)
en.%markerMenu_marker_m_description%: Paragraph text, with no first line indent (may occur after poetry) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mi_description%: Paragraph text, indented, with no first line indent; often used for discourse
en.%markerMenu_marker_mr_description%: A major section division references range heading (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ms_description%: A major section division heading, level 1 (if single level) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ms1_description%: A major section division heading, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_ms2_description%: A major section division heading, level 2
en.%markerMenu_marker_ms3_description%: A major section division heading, level 3
en.%markerMenu_marker_mt_description%: The main title of the book (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mt1_description%: The main title of the book (if multiple levels) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mt2_description%: A secondary title usually occurring before the main title (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mt3_description%: A secondary title occurring after the main title
en.%markerMenu_marker_mt4_description%: A small secondary title sometimes occurring within parentheses
en.%markerMenu_marker_mte_description%: The main title of the book repeated at the end of the book, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mte1_description%: The main title of the book repeated at the end of the book, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_mte2_description%: A secondary title occurring before or after the 'ending' main title
en.%markerMenu_marker_nb_description%: Paragraph text, with no break from previous paragraph text (at chapter boundary) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_no_description%: A character style, use normal text
en.%markerMenu_marker_ord_description%: For the text portion of an ordinal number
en.%markerMenu_marker_p_description%: Paragraph text, with first line indent (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_pb_description%: Page Break used for new reader portions and children's bibles where content is controlled by the page
en.%markerMenu_marker_pi_description%: Paragraph text, level 1 indent (if single level), with first line indent; often used for discourse (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_pi1_description%: Paragraph text, level 1 indent (if multiple levels), with first line indent; often used for discourse
en.%markerMenu_marker_pi2_description%: Paragraph text, level 2 indent, with first line indent; often used for discourse
en.%markerMenu_marker_pi3_description%: Paragraph text, level 3 indent, with first line indent; often used for discourse
en.%markerMenu_marker_pmr_description%: Embedded text refrain (e.g. Then all the people shall say, 'Amen!')
en.%markerMenu_marker_q_description%: Poetry text, level 1 indent (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_q1_description%: Poetry text, level 1 indent (if multiple levels) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_qac_description%: Poetry text, Acrostic markup of the first character of a line of acrostic poetry
en.%markerMenu_marker_qd_description%: A Hebrew musical performance annotation, similar in content to Hebrew descriptive title.
en.%markerMenu_marker_qm_description%: Poetry text, embedded, level 1 indent (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_qm1_description%: Poetry text, embedded, level 1 indent (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_qt_description%: For Old Testament quoted text appearing in the New Testament (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_rq_description%: A cross-reference indicating the source text for the preceding quotation.
en.%markerMenu_marker_s_description%: A section heading, level 1 (if single level) (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_s1_description%: A section heading, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_s3_description%: A section heading, level 3 (e.g. Genesis 'The First Day')
en.%markerMenu_marker_sc_description%: A character style, for small capitalization text
en.%markerMenu_marker_sd_description%: Vertical space used to divide the text into sections, level 1 (if single level)
en.%markerMenu_marker_sd1_description%: Vertical space used to divide the text into sections, level 1 (if multiple levels)
en.%markerMenu_marker_sd2_description%: Vertical space used to divide the text into sections, level 2
en.%markerMenu_marker_sd3_description%: Vertical space used to divide the text into sections, level 3
en.%markerMenu_marker_sd4_description%: Vertical space used to divide the text into sections, level 4
en.%markerMenu_marker_sig_description%: For the signature of the author of an Epistle
en.%markerMenu_marker_sls_description%: To represent where the original text is in a secondary language or from an alternate text source
en.%markerMenu_marker_sp_description%: A heading, to identify the speaker (e.g. Job)
en.%markerMenu_marker_sr_description%: A section division references range heading
en.%markerMenu_marker_sup_description%: A character style, for superscript text. Typically for use in critical edition footnotes.
en.%markerMenu_marker_toca1_description%: Alternative language long table of contents text
en.%markerMenu_marker_toca2_description%: Alternative language short table of contents text
en.%markerMenu_marker_va_description%: Second (alternate) verse number (for coding dual numeration in Psalms; see also NRSV Exo 22.1-4)
en.%markerMenu_marker_vp_description%: Published verse marker (verse string that should appear in the published text)
en.%markerMenu_marker_wj_description%: For marking the words of Jesus
en.%markerMenu_marker_xnt_description%: Cross-reference target reference(s), New Testament only
en.%markerMenu_marker_xop_description%: Published cross reference origin reference (origin reference that should appear in the published text)
en.%markerMenu_marker_xot_description%: Cross-reference target reference(s), Old Testament only
en.%markerMenu_marker_xq_description%: A cross-reference quotation from the scripture text
en.%markerMenu_marker_xt_description%: The cross reference target reference(s), protocanon only (basic)
en.%markerMenu_marker_xta_description%: Cross reference target references added text
en.%paragraphMenu_m_markerDescription%: Paragraph - Margin - No First Line Indent
en.%paragraphMenu_nb_markerDescription%: Paragraph - No Break with Previous Paragraph
en.%paragraphMenu_pi_markerDescription%: Paragraph - Indented - Level 1 - First Line Indent
en.%paragraphMenu_toc1_markerDescription%: File - Long Table of Contents Text
en.%paragraphMenu_toc2_markerDescription%: File - Short Table of Contents Text

es.%markerMenu_marker_add_description%: Para una adición traduccional al texto
es.%markerMenu_marker_addpn_description%: Para palabras en chino con subrayado punteado y subrayado
es.%markerMenu_marker_b_description%: Salto de estrofa en texto poético (por ejemplo, separación de estrofas) (básico)
es.%markerMenu_marker_bd_description%: Estilo de carácter, usar texto en negrita
es.%markerMenu_marker_bdit_description%: Estilo de carácter, usar texto en negrita y cursiva
es.%markerMenu_marker_bk_description%: Para el nombre citado de un libro
es.%markerMenu_marker_ca_description%: Segundo número de capítulo (alternativo) para doble versificación
es.%markerMenu_marker_cd_description%: Descripción del capítulo (opción de publicación D)
es.%markerMenu_marker_cl_description%: Etiqueta de capítulo usada antes del número de capítulo
es.%markerMenu_marker_fdc_description%: Texto de nota al pie (solo Deuterocanónicos)
es.%markerMenu_marker_fk_description%: Palabra clave de nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_fm_description%: Marcador adicional de nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_fp_description%: Párrafo adicional en nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_fq_description%: Cita bíblica en nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_fqa_description%: Traducción alternativa en nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_fr_description%: Referencia de origen de nota al pie

es.%markerMenu_marker_fw_description%: Lista de testigos en nota al pie
es.%markerMenu_marker_imq_description%: Párrafo introductorio citado sin sangría inicial
es.%markerMenu_marker_ipr_description%: Párrafo introductorio alineado a la derecha
es.%markerMenu_marker_litl_description%: Texto total de entrada de lista
es.%markerMenu_marker_mi_description%: Párrafo con sangría sin sangría inicial
es.%markerMenu_marker_mr_description%: Encabezado de referencias de sección mayor
es.%markerMenu_marker_mte_description%: Título principal repetido al final nivel 1
es.%markerMenu_marker_mte1_description%: Título principal repetido al final nivel 1 (múltiple)
es.%markerMenu_marker_nb_description%: Párrafo sin salto en límite de capítulo
es.%markerMenu_marker_qr_description%: Texto poético alineado a la derecha
es.%markerMenu_marker_qt_description%: Cita del Antiguo Testamento en el Nuevo Testamento
es.%markerMenu_marker_sls_description%: Texto en idioma secundario u origen alternativo
es.%markerMenu_marker_sr_description%: Encabezado de rango de referencias de sección
es.%markerMenu_marker_toc1_description%: Texto largo de tabla de contenido
es.%markerMenu_marker_toc2_description%: Texto corto de tabla de contenido
es.%markerMenu_marker_toca1_description%: Texto largo alternativo de tabla de contenido
